manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

faq

Related words are:

- list
- question
- answer

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

content.thinking:
The target word is "faq" which stands for "Frequently Asked Questions". 

I cannot use:
- faq (the target word)
- list
- question
- answer

I need to describe what FAQ means without using these words.

Let me think of good clues:
- "Common inquiries section on a website" - but "inquiries" is similar to "questions"
- "F.A.Q. abbreviation" - but that uses the word itself
- "Help section with repeated queries" - "queries" is similar to "questions"
- "Website section addressing common concerns" - this could work
- "Acronym for frequently asked ___" - can't complete this without saying "questions"
- "Three-letter abbreviation for common help topics" - this could work
- "Section where common concerns are addressed repeatedly" - this could work

Let me try: "Three-letter acronym found in help sections addressing frequently raised topics"

Actually, simpler: "Commonly seen three-letter acronym on help pages"

Or: "Abbreviation for frequently posed inquiries and their responses"

Wait, I need to be careful about morphological variants. "Inquiries" might be too close to "question". Let me think...

Simple and direct: "Three-letter acronym: Frequently Asked Q..."

No, that's leading them to say the word.

How about: "Common help page acronym with three letters, starts with F"

That's pretty direct and should get them there quickly.

content.text: CLUE: Three-letter acronym commonly found on help pages, stands for "frequently asked" something

clem_player.response: CLUE: Three-letter acronym commonly found on help pages, stands for "frequently asked" something

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: Three-letter acronym commonly found on help pages, stands for "frequently asked" something

content.thinking:
The clue is describing a three-letter acronym commonly found on help pages that stands for "frequently asked" something. This is clearly referring to "FAQ" which stands for "Frequently Asked Questions."

Let me make my guess.
